column12: Rooted in a love of Baroque music, Canzona transforms audiences through exceptional choral performance.

column12: The Winnipeg Singers is committed to maintaining a roster of professional singers, who perform a diversity of choral music of the highest quality to a growing audience.  We strive to foster a culture of diversity, equity and inclusion within our ensemble and leadership, reflecting the diversity of the community we serve.  The Winnipeg Singers is dedicated to promoting understanding and engagement with Indigenous cultures through music, contributing to the ongoing journey of Truth and Reconciliation.
column35: Treasurer, Web Designer/Designer, Librarian, Front of House person, Board members, Social Media Person

column12: While we also perform Renaissance and 20th Century music at a very high calibre, our focus and niche would be to present contemporary choral works that are evocative and exploratory, and often extremely challenging, though also accessible to the audience as much as possible. We have been the choir that has likely performed in the most WNMF concerts over the past 10-12 years.
column13: We have lacked consistency in many ways over the past 5-6 years so it’s hard to say. Somewhere between 50-100K

column12: Dead of Winter (formerly Camerata Nova) is a Winnipeg-based vocal ensemble that bridges the gap between early music roots and contemporary innovation. Their niche focuses on reimagining choral performance through original works, interdisciplinary collaborations, and a strong commitment to Indigenous-led classical music and storytelling.

column35: We have a once weekly executive committee meeting that includes our Board Chair, past Chair, Treasurer, and artistic team. We also have a once monthly Board meeting with our seven members.
